models.processStep: A single step in the wastewater-to-clean-water flow, with icon, title, short description, and step number.
models.comparisonPoint: A single point of comparison between JCA's approach and traditional Philippine wastewater treatment.
models.technologyPillar: One of the core technology areas: biological treatment, modular design, solar/IoT, or scalability.
models.liveMetric: A dashboard metric card with current value, label, period, and visual chart data.
models.chartDataPoint: A single data point for chart visualization: label (date/month) and numeric value.

relationships: processStep entries are ordered sequentially (step 1 → step N).
comparisonPoint entries are standalone comparison rows.
technologyPillar entries are standalone — grid display.
liveMetric entries drive the horizontal-scrolling dashboard cards.
chartDataPoint is embedded within each liveMetric.

sectionSubtitle: Nature-mimicking technology that treats water without the cost and footprint of conventional plants.

steps.label: Collection & Screening
steps.description: Raw wastewater and septage arrive at the facility. Screens remove large solids — plastics, debris, grit — before treatment begins.

steps.label: Biological Treatment
steps.description: In the Sequential Batch Reactor, naturally occurring microorganisms consume organic pollutants. The same nitrogen cycle that keeps rivers and aquariums healthy — engineered for city scale.

steps.label: Clarification
steps.description: Treated water passes through settling tanks where remaining particles sink and are separated. Clear water rises to the top, ready for final polishing.

steps.label: UV Disinfection
steps.description: Water flows through ultraviolet light chambers that neutralize remaining pathogens. Solar panels help power this final sterilization step — no chemicals, no chlorine taste.

steps.label: Clean Water Release
steps.description: The recycled water is released back into the bay or river. It's clean enough for marine life to thrive — and at Puerto Princesa, it now hosts Ironman triathlons and dragon boat races.

comparison.title: A Different Approach
comparison.subtitle: Why JCA 1221's model works where traditional systems fail

points.label: Treatment method
points.jca: Biological — microorganisms do the work, mimicking natural water cycles

points.label: Cost structure
points.jca: Affordable for small municipalities. Public-private partnership model shares risk.
points.traditional: High capital and operating costs. Often too expensive for mid-size cities, let alone island communities.

points.label: Energy use
points.jca: Solar-assisted operations reduce grid dependence. Modular design minimizes pumping needs.
points.traditional: Fully grid-dependent. Power costs can be 30–40% of operating budget.

points.label: Design approach
points.jca: Modular, phased deployment. Start small, scale with demand. Fits any terrain.
points.traditional: Monolithic, one-size-fits-all plants. Require flat land, large footprint, and long construction timelines.

points.label: Transparency
points.jca: IoT remote monitoring. Real-time water quality data available to partners and the public.
points.traditional: Periodic manual sampling. Data often months old. Limited public access.

points.label: Community integration
points.jca: Every facility includes a Learning Center. Local operators are trained as employees.
points.traditional: Facility is a black box. Community has no visibility or economic stake.

technologyPillars.title: Biological Nutrient Removal
technologyPillars.description: Bacteria break down pollutants — the same nitrogen cycle that keeps rivers and aquariums healthy. No chemicals. No toxic byproducts. Biology guided by engineering.

technologyPillars.title: Modular, Phased Design
technologyPillars.description: Prefabricated treatment units allow rapid deployment in phases. Start with the capacity a community needs today, add modules as it grows — no expensive upfront overbuilding. Works on coastal terrain, hillsides, and small island footprints.

technologyPillars.title: Solar-Assisted & IoT Connected
technologyPillars.description: Solar panels reduce grid energy consumption. IoT sensors stream real-time water quality data to a cloud dashboard — partners and regulators can monitor performance from anywhere. Remote diagnostics catch issues before they become problems.

technologyPillars.title: Scalable from City to Island
technologyPillars.description: The same technology stack that treats 4,000 cubic meters daily in Puerto Princesa works at 2,500 cubic meters in Gingoog and 15 tons of solid waste in Del Carmen. The model adapts without compromising on quality, transparency, or affordability.

liveMetrics.title: Live Impact Dashboard
liveMetrics.subtitle: Real-time treatment data from active JCA 1221 facilities

metrics.label: Daily Treatment Volume (30-Day Average)
metrics.context: 4.3% above design capacity — within healthy operating range

metrics.label: Solar Energy Contribution
metrics.context: Of total facility energy from solar PV — reducing costs and carbon footprint
